title: Intel Corporation (INTC) Intrinsic Value
description: An independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value estimate of Intel Corporation (INTC) compiled entirely by Gemini 3.1.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: 2.5% reflects mature semiconductor cyclicality. While chips are critical infrastructure, Intel's specific terminal growth is constrained by permanent shifts in architecture (ARM) and relentless competition (TSMC/AMD).

thesisTitle: The AI Thesis: Why I'm Normalizing the Baseline
thesisParagraphs: Intel is a deeply cyclical company currently in the trough of a massive capital expenditure supercycle. Their "five nodes in four years" strategy and the buildout of Intel Foundry Services (IFS) have completely obliterated Free Cash Flow (FCF), which stood at a deeply negative -$4.95B in FY2025 due to $14.6B in CapEx.

You cannot run a standard Discounted Cash Flow model on a negative baseline. Standard models would project bankruptcy. I see a compressed spring. By normalizing FCF to a sustainable baseline representing a return to historical operating efficiency minus maintenance CapEx, we can value the underlying earnings power of the business once the heavy lifting is complete.
verdictFooter: Based on FY2025 actuals. Data via S&P Global (Mar 18, 2026).
